generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LX2DLR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX 2D LiDAR sensor providing raw point cloud data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointsRawTrans
]

instance WheelEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Wheel encoder sensor providing vehicle odometry data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateRawTrans
]

instance IMU : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Inertial Measurement Unit providing vehicle attitude and acceleration data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateRawTrans
]

instance SteeringServo : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo position sensor providing steering angle data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringServoRawTrans
]

instance MotorStatus : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor status sensor providing motor speed and torque data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStatusRawTrans
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointsRawTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LX2DLR
]

instance VehicleStateRawTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom WheelEncoder, IMU
]

instance SteeringServoRawTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServo
]

instance MotorStatusRawTrans : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStatus
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARPointsRawData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data collected from Hokuyo UST-10LX."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PerceptionEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointsRawTrans
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle state data comprising speed and odometry from wheel encoders and IMU."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCExecutionEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateRawTrans
]

instance SteeringServoData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering angle data from the steering servo."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCExecutionEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringServoRawTrans
]

instance MotorStatusData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor status data including speed and torque from the motor controller."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCExecutionEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorStatusRawTrans
]

instance PointCloudTrackingData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Clustered object tracks extracted from raw LiDAR point cloud via DBSCAN and Kalman filtering."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PerceptionEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointsRawTrans
]

instance DesiredSpeedCommand : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Desired longitudinal speed calculated by the Adaptive Cruise Control model."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCExecutionEnabler
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateRawTrans
]

// C9: Models
instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Perception model performing geometric clustering with DBSCAN on LiDAR data and tracking via Kalman filter."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PerceptionEnabler
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Control model consuming vehicle state data to compute longitudinal control commands for ACC."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCExecutionEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PerceptionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS 2 node implementing perception FEs and handling point cloud clustering & tracking."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ACCExecutionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS 2 node implementing ACC FEs and performing control logic."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Realtime data monitoring service displaying vehicle state, projected path, and object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:provides InsightVehicleStateView, ActionSaveHistoricalReport, ActionViewHistoricData
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offload ACC computation to the DT cloud and transmit desired speed back to the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionTransmitDesiredSpeed
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote management service enabling operators to control ACC via UI."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActionEnableACC, ActionSetTargetSpeed, ActionEmergencyBrake
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance InsightVehicleStateView : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "View of real-time vehicle state data in the 3D visualization UI."
]

instance ActionSaveHistoricalReport : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generate and save a historic state data report."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionViewHistoricData : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "View historic state data and analytic insights."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionTransmitDesiredSpeed : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Transmit desired longitudinal speed command from the DT to the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActionEnableACC : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enable or disable the Adaptive Cruise Control function."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionSetTargetSpeed : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Set a target speed for the ACC system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ActionEmergencyBrake : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issue an emergency braking command through the UI."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands generated by the digital twin are issued to the physical twin through the Actuation functional entity (FE). The ACC Controlling FE computes a desired speed based on lead‑vehicle detection and sends a speed command, while remote‑management functions (enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, emergency‑brake) are also transmitted. Commands are serialized by the Data Translation FE and transmitted over the same Wi‑Fi UDP channel used for sensor data. Upon receipt, the Actuation FE drives the motor and steering‑servo to realise the commanded actions. Explicit acknowledgement or confirmation of command execution is not detailed in the documents."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twin requires a shared notion of time across devices; synchronization accuracy of 100 ms is mandated (hardware‑limited) and complies with safety‑critical vehicular standards that often demand tighter (<100 ms) sync. This 100 ms bound governs the coordination needed for computation off‑loading between the physical and digital components."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "Orchestration is built on the ISO 23247 reference architecture; software components are mapped to Functional Entities and each FE is packaged as an individual ROS 2 package containing one or more ROS 2 nodes. Communication and coordination among the three entities occur via ROS 2 middleware, allowing distributed execution across heterogeneous compute resources."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The architecture requires that all devices share a common notion of time with a synchronization accuracy of 100 ms, as stated: many standards mandate stricter clock synchronization than 100 ms for safety‑critical vehicular applications, but the system limits to 100 ms accuracy due to hardware limitations."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DTS employs a two‑layer network architecture. Internally, software components exchange messages on a ROS2 (DDS) network, providing publish‑subscribe communication within the digital environment. Communication between the physical twin and the digital twin occurs over a Wi‑Fi network using UDP at the transport layer. The system is required to support low end‑to‑end latency to satisfy safety‑critical ACC operation, although specific latency or bandwidth figures are not provided. No explicit reliability, redundancy, or security protocols are described in the supplied material."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "ACC computation is offloaded from the vehicle’s resource‑constrained ECU to the Digital Twin, which can run on cloud resources as indicated by the statement that computation is allocated to 'better‑suited equipment, e.g., in the cloud.' No further details on the hosting platform, computational or storage capacity, scalability mechanisms, deployment models, or availability guarantees are given in the documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Interoperability follows ISO 23247 part 2 and may employ standards such as OPC Unified Architecture for data exchange, although the present implementation uses ROS 2 topics/services. The design therefore supports integration with external enterprise systems or other digital twins through OPC UA or ROS 2 bridges, aligning with the standard’s information‑exchange guidelines."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is built on the ISO 23247 Digital Twin framework for manufacturing. Specifically, Part 2 of ISO 23247, which defines the entity‑based reference architecture (Observable Manufacturing Element, Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, User Entity) is used as the primary design basis. The implementation follows the ISO‑defined entities but introduces a Physical Twin Entity that extends the standard’s OME concept. Functional Entities are instantiated as ROS 2 packages, aligning with the standard’s allowance for arbitrary FE implementation (e.g., OPC UA, ROS 2). The design acknowledges deviations from the ISO model and notes their impact on ownership and engineering processes."
]
